notable_pick_trades: Projected to receive 4 compensatory picks (two 5th-rounders, two 7th-rounders) per OverTheCap. No major traded picks. Have LAC 5th-rounder (No. 160) from Odafe Oweh trade.
roster_context: Coming off a hugely disappointing 8-9 season that led to John Harbaugh's firing after 18 years. Jesse Minter hired from the Chargers as the new defensive-minded HC. Lamar Jackson's $74.5M cap hit must be restructured/extended to unlock roster-building flexibility. The defense collapsed without Nnamdi Madubuike (neck injury, 15 games missed), finishing 30th in the NFL in sacks (30). The offensive interior line was a consistent weakness, and the Ravens face 19 unrestricted free agents including elite center Tyler Linderbaum, TE Isaiah Likely, EDGE Kyle Van Noy, and DE Dre'Mont Jones. The team is in retool mode with a new coaching staff but still has Lamar Jackson, Derrick Henry, Kyle Hamilton, Roquan Smith, and Zay Flowers as a strong core.

needs.explanation: The Ravens finished 30th in the NFL in sacks (30) and 29th in pressure rate (32.1%) in 2025. Kyle Van Noy (34, UFA) collapsed from 12.5 sacks in 2024 to just 2 in 2025. Dre'Mont Jones (UFA) and David Ojabo (UFA) are also departing. The only under-contract edge rushers are 2025 second-rounder Mike Green (3.5 sacks), Tavius Robinson (4.5 sacks), and Adisa Isaac (barely played). This is a deep edge class and DeCosta has signaled the draft is the priority vehicle for this fix. Trey Hendrickson and Khalil Mack have been mentioned as FA targets.
needs.current_starter: Mike Green, Tavius Robinson (Kyle Van Noy UFA)
needs.depth: Adisa Isaac (4 games in 2 seasons), no other proven options

needs.explanation: Tyler Linderbaum (3x Pro Bowl C) is an unrestricted free agent seeking $25M/year — contract talks are stalled and he may leave. Daniel Faalele (RG, UFA) was the 52nd-rated guard per PFF and widely criticized. Andrew Vorhees (LG) has struggled and is a cut candidate. If Linderbaum departs, the Ravens could need three new interior OL starters. Emery Jones Jr. (2025 3rd-round pick) is the only young internal option and projects to fill one guard spot.
needs.current_starter: Tyler Linderbaum (C, UFA), Andrew Vorhees (LG), Daniel Faalele (RG, UFA)
needs.depth: Emery Jones Jr. (G), Corey Bullock (C backup), Carson Vinson (developmental)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 for a guard; backup C plan if Linderbaum leaves

needs.explanation: Nnamdi Madubuike suffered a potentially career-threatening neck injury in Week 2 and missed 15 games. His status for 2026 remains highly uncertain despite some positive signals. He carries a $31M cap hit. Broderick Washington (Achilles surgery) is a cut candidate ($4M savings). Travis Jones (extended) and John Jenkins (extended) are the only reliable pieces. The Ravens had the NFL's 2nd-lowest sack rate (4.6%) and desperately need interior pass-rush reinforcement regardless of Madubuike's return.
needs.depth: John Jenkins (veteran NT), Broderick Washington (Achilles, cut candidate), Aeneas Peebles
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3, high priority for a 3-tech pass rusher

needs.explanation: DeAndre Hopkins (33, UFA) is departing after a career-low 330 yards. Rashod Bateman had a career-low 224 yards in 2025 and is the current WR2 but underperformed badly. Zay Flowers is elite (back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons) but has no reliable complement. Devontez Walker and LaJohntay Wester are unproven depth. Baltimore needs a true outside WR to pair with Flowers. Multiple mock drafts have the Ravens targeting a WR in the first two rounds.
needs.depth: Devontez Walker, LaJohntay Wester, Cornelius Johnson — very thin

needs.explanation: Marlon Humphrey is expected to return but is coming off a poor 2025, is entering his age-30 season, and carries a $26.3M cap hit that may require a restructure or pay cut. Nate Wiggins is the future CB1 entering Year 3. Chidobe Awuzie (UFA) is departing. Behind Wiggins and Humphrey, only T.J. Tampa and Keyon Martin remain, neither of whom have received significant playing time. The Ravens need a true CB2 long-term, especially with Humphrey aging and in a contract year.
needs.depth: T.J. Tampa, Keyon Martin — very limited experience

needs.explanation: Isaiah Likely (UFA) appears headed out of Baltimore after a disappointing season (27 catches, 307 yards). Charlie Kolar (UFA) is also departing. Mark Andrews signed a 3-year extension but had a down 2025. The Ravens may need to draft a TE to develop as the future TE2/eventual replacement for the aging Andrews (33 in 2026).
needs.depth: No TEs under contract beyond Andrews after Likely and Kolar leave

needs.explanation: Kyle Hamilton is an All-Pro locked up long-term. Malaki Starks (2025 1st-round pick) is the FS alongside Hamilton. However, Alohi Gilman (UFA) and Ar'Darius Washington (UFA) are both departing, leaving a void at the third safety/sub-package role. The Ravens need a third safety to complement Hamilton and Starks.
needs.depth: Thin after losing Gilman and Washington in FA

needs.explanation: Roquan Smith remains the anchor but had a down year and carries a $32.7M cap hit. Trenton Simpson has the ILB2 job but hasn't progressed as hoped. Jay Higgins IV is a developmental UDFA. The Ravens could use more depth and a long-term plan next to Smith, but it isn't a top-3 need.
needs.depth: Jay Higgins IV, Carl Jones Jr. (ERFA)

free_agency_impact: Tyler Linderbaum (C, UFA) — contract negotiations stalled, seeking $25M/year; Ravens offered 'market-setting' deal but can't tag him. May leave, which would make IOL a catastrophic need
Isaiah Likely (TE, UFA) — expected to depart per multiple reports, leaving only Mark Andrews at TE
Charlie Kolar (TE, UFA) — run-blocking specialist also departing, deepens TE need
Kyle Van Noy (EDGE, UFA, 34) — collapsed from 12.5 to 2 sacks, departing. Major edge loss
Dre'Mont Jones (DE, UFA) — led Ravens in pressures after midseason acquisition, likely leaving
David Ojabo (EDGE, UFA) — 0.5 sacks in 2025, departing
DeAndre Hopkins (WR, UFA, 33) — contract voided, leaving. Removes WR3 option
Chidobe Awuzie (CB, UFA) — solid 2025 season, likely departing. Weakens CB depth significantly
Daniel Faalele (RG, UFA) — 52nd-rated guard, likely not re-signed. Opens RG spot
Alohi Gilman (S, UFA) — third safety role vacant without him
Ar'Darius Washington (S, UFA) — versatile DB role vacant
Marlon Humphrey — DeCosta confirmed he expects Humphrey to return despite $26.3M cap hit; may restructure
Nnamdi Madubuike — reports suggest he intends to return from neck injury but status remains officially uncertain; $31M cap hit guaranteed
Lamar Jackson — extension/restructure expected before March 11 to reduce $74.5M cap hit and unlock ~$30M+ in space
Derrick Henry — under contract through 2027 via extension ($11M guaranteed in 2026); RB not a need
Mark Andrews — signed 3-year, $39.3M extension in December 2025; TE1 locked in
Ravens targeting Trey Hendrickson, Khalil Mack, or Maxx Crosby trade as veteran EDGE additions
scheme_fit_notes: New HC Jesse Minter brings a defensive-minded approach from the Chargers. Minter's system utilizes match coverages in the secondary (Hamilton/Starks are perfect fits) and demands versatile interior DL who can rush the passer and defend the run on all downs. Edge rushers in Minter's system are asked to do more than just pin-their-ears-back rushing — they must set edges and contribute in multiple responsibilities, meaning high-floor, versatile EDGE prospects have elevated value. Minter has emphasized building through the trenches. On offense, the Ravens run a heavy ground-and-pound scheme with Lamar Jackson's dual-threat ability, making IOL who can dominate in gap/power run blocking particularly valuable. WR prospects who can win contested catches and provide a big-bodied outside target are ideal complements to the undersized slot talent of Zay Flowers.
